title: Shuttle Drivers and Chauffeurs
description: Drive a motor vehicle to transport passengers on a planned or scheduled basis. May collect a fare. Includes nonemergency medical transporters and hearse drivers.

tasks.text: Test vehicle equipment, such as lights, brakes, horns, or windshield wipers, to ensure proper operation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check the condition of a vehicle's tires, brakes, windshield wipers, lights, oil, fuel, water, and safety equipment to ensure that everything is in working order.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Comply with traffic regulations to operate vehicles in a safe and courteous manner.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Follow relevant safety regulations and state laws governing vehicle operation, and ensure that passengers follow safety regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate vehicles with specialized equipment, such as wheelchair lifts, to transport and secure passengers with special needs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Report any vehicle malfunctions or needed repairs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform routine vehicle maintenance, such as regulating tire pressure and adding gasoline, oil, and water.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Pick up and drop off passengers at regularly scheduled neighborhood locations, following strict time schedules.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drive shuttle busses, limousines, company cars, or privately owned vehicles to transport passengers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare and submit reports that may include the number of passengers or trips, hours worked, mileage driven fuel consumed, or fares received.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Notify dispatchers or company mechanics of vehicle problems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide passengers with assistance entering and exiting vehicles, and help them with any luggage.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Communicate with dispatchers by radio, telephone, or computer to exchange information and receive requests for passenger service.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Pick up or meet passengers according to requests, appointments, or schedules.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Arrange to pick up particular customers or groups on a regular schedule.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Report delays, accidents, or other traffic and transportation situations, using telephones or mobile two-way radios.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Complete accident reports when necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Read maps and follow written and verbal geographic directions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Maintain knowledge of first-aid procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Regulate heating, lighting, and ventilation systems for passenger comfort.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record vehicle routes.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Vacuum and clean interiors, and wash and polish exteriors of automobiles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide passengers with information or advice about the local area, points of interest, hotels, or restaurants.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Collect fares or vouchers from passengers, and make change or issue receipts as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Perform minor vehicle repairs, such as cleaning spark plugs, or take vehicles to mechanics for servicing.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform errands for customers or employers, such as delivering or picking up mail and packages.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
